priority_plan.name: scaffolded_readout_vs_baseline_intervention
priority_plan.why_now: still directly targets object_to_readout_compatibility and remains compatible with long-chain constraints

priority_plan.name: reasoning_slice_transport_intervention
priority_plan.why_now: still the best immediate companion to the winner while keeping family-conditioned shared reasoning slice intact

priority_plan.name: stage_conditioned_reasoning_transport_intervention
priority_plan.why_now: now promoted because long-chain inventory shows stage structure must be represented explicitly

priority_plan.name: causal_successor_alignment_intervention
priority_plan.why_now: now promoted because long-chain inventory introduces successor coherence as a new survival constraint

priority_plan.name: stress_guard_intervention

priority_plan.name: anchored_relation_lift_intervention

verdict.core_answer: Long-chain constraints now force the joint P3/P4 program into a six-step priority plan where stage-conditioned transport and successor alignment move ahead of later stress and bridge interventions.
verdict.next_engineering_target: execute priorities 1-4 as one coherent long-chain constrained block rather than as isolated intervention tests.
